title: tinus × montana
intro: Tinus × montana, also known as the hybrid of Gaultheria shallon and Gaultheria procumbens, is a rare shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues, including coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Tinus × montana include its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to ease menstrual cramps and soothe irritated skin. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
